# Getting started with brms

# Installation

Before installing brms, update R to the latest available version. This will ensure that all our libraries will be compatible with each other. Installing
brms with an older version of R may work, but is likely to cause weird issues
later.

`brms`'s power and flexibility require some special preparations, but the first step is easy: install brms with

```{r install brms}
#| eval: false
install.packages("brms")
```

 The first
step is to install a special R library called `devtools`.
